request.description:
## HealthCheck

A simple health check endpoint to confirm whether the RESTful Booker API is up and running. Use this endpoint to verify service availability before executing other requests.

---

## Endpoint

| Property | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Method** | `GET` |
| **URL** | `{{restful-booker_url}}/ping` |
| **Authentication** | None |

---

## Request Details

### Headers

No custom headers required.

### Query Parameters

None.

### Request Body

None.

---

## Variables Used

| Variable | Scope | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `{{restful-booker_url}}` | Collection | Base URL of the RESTful Booker API |
| `{{max_response_time}}` | Collection | Maximum acceptable response time in milliseconds (used in tests) |

---

## Response

### Success — `201 Created`

Returned when the API is healthy and running.

| Property | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Status Code** | `201 Created` |
| **Content-Type** | `text/plain` |
| **Body** | `Created` |

**Example response body:**

```
Created

 ```

> ⚠️ Note: The API returns `201 Created` (not `200 OK`) for a successful health check. This is the expected behavior of the RESTful Booker API. 
  

---

## Automated Tests

This request includes the following post-response test scripts:

| Test | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Test_1** | Asserts the response status code is `201 Created` |
| **Test_2** | Asserts the response body exactly equals `"Created"` |
| **Test_3** | Asserts the response time is below the `{{max_response_time}}` environment variable value |

---

## Usage

Run this request at the start of a collection run or before any booking operations to confirm the API is reachable. If this request fails, subsequent requests in the collection are likely to fail as well.

## Overview

This folder contains the health check endpoint for the RESTful Booker API. Its purpose is to verify that the API is up and running before executing any other requests. It should be run at the start of every collection run or workflow to confirm service availability.

---

## Requests in this Folder

| # | Name | Method | Endpoint | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | HealthCheck | GET | `{{restful-booker_url}}/ping` | Confirms the API is healthy and reachable |

---

## Expected Behavior

A successful health check returns a `201 Created` status code with a plain text response body of `Created`.

> ⚠️ **Note:** The API returns `201 Created` (not `200 OK`) for a successful health check. This is the expected and intentional behavior of the RESTful Booker API. 
  

---

## Automated Tests

The following tests are run automatically on the HealthCheck request after each response:

| Test | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Test_1 | Asserts the response status code is `201 Created` |
| Test_2 | Asserts the response body exactly equals `"Created"` |
| Test_3 | Asserts the response time is below the `{{max_response_time}}` collection variable value |

---

## Usage Guidance

Run this folder or the HealthCheck request at the start of a collection run. If this request fails, subsequent requests in the collection are likely to fail as well.

request.description:
# Auth - CreateToken

This request authenticates a user against the RESTful Booker API and retrieves a session token. The token is required to perform protected operations — specifically the **PUT** and **PATCH** (update booking), **DELETE** (delete booking) endpoints under `/booking`.

---

## Endpoint

```
POST {{base_url}}/auth

 ```

---

## Authentication

No authentication is required for this request. It is the entry point for obtaining a token that will be used to authenticate subsequent requests.

---

## Request Headers

| Header | Value |
| --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` |

---

## Request Body

The request body must be a JSON object containing valid credentials.

``` json
{
  "username": "{{valid_username}}",
  "password": "{{valid_password}}"
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Variable | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `username` | string | `{{valid_username}}` | The username of the account to authenticate with |
| `password` | string | `{{valid_password}}` | The password of the account to authenticate with |

---

## Response

### 200 OK

A successful response returns a JSON object containing a single `token` property — an alphanumeric string used to authorize PUT and DELETE requests.

``` json
{
  "token": "abc123def456"
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `token` | string | Token for use in protected booking endpoints |

---

## Variables

Upon a successful `200` response, the `token` value is **automatically extracted and saved** to the `token` collection variable. This makes it immediately available for use in subsequent requests such as:

- `PUT /booking/:id` and `PATCH /booking/:id`— Update an existing booking
    
- `DELETE /booking/:id` — Delete an existing booking
    

The token is stored via the post-response script:

``` javascript
pm.collectionVariables.set("token", resBody.token);

 ```

---

## Notes

- **Response time validation**: The response time is automatically validated against the `max_response_time` collection variable.
    
- The token format is validated to be a non-empty alphanumeric string matching `/^[a-zA-Z0-9]+$/`.
    
- The response body is validated to be a JSON object containing exactly one key: `token`.
    

---

## Automated Tests

This request includes the following post-response test scripts:

| Test | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Test_1** | Asserts the response status code is `200 OK` |
| **Test_2** | Asserts the response time is below the `{{max_response_time}}` collection variable value |
| **Test_3** | Asserts the response body is a JSON object with exactly one key: `token` |
| **Test_4** | Asserts the `token` property is a non-empty alphanumeric string, and saves it to the `token` collection variable |

# Auth - CreateToken (Invalid Credentials)

This request attempts to authenticate with invalid credentials against the RESTful Booker API. The API returns a `200 OK` status even for invalid credentials, but instead of a token, the response contains a `reason` field with the value `"Bad credentials"`. The `token` collection variable is cleared (set to empty string) to prevent stale tokens from being used in subsequent requests.

---

## Endpoint

```
POST {{base_url}}/auth

 ```

---

## Authentication

No authentication is required for this request.

---

## Request Headers

| Header | Value |
| --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` |

---

## Request Body

JSON object with invalid credentials:

``` json
{
  "username": "{{invalid_username}}",
  "password": "{{invalid_password}}"
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Variable | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `username` | string | `{{invalid_username}}` | An invalid username that does not match any account |
| `password` | string | `{{invalid_password}}` | An invalid password that does not match any account |

---

## Response

### 200 OK

Despite invalid credentials, the API returns `200 OK`. The response body contains a single `reason` field instead of a token.

``` json
{
  "reason": "Bad credentials"
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `reason` | string | Explains why authentication failed. Value is always `"Bad credentials"` for invalid credentials |

---

## Variables

Upon receiving the response, the `token` collection variable is **cleared** (set to an empty string) to ensure no stale token from a previous successful authentication is accidentally used in subsequent requests.

---

## Notes

- The API does **not** return a `4xx` error for invalid credentials — it always returns `200 OK`.
    
- Response time is validated against the `max_response_time` collection variable.
    
- The response body is validated to be a JSON object with exactly one key: `reason`.
    

---

## Automated Tests

| Test | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Test_1** | Asserts the response status code is `200 OK` (API returns 200 even for invalid credentials) |
| **Test_2** | Asserts the response time is below the `{{max_response_time}}` collection variable value |
| **Test_3** | Asserts the response body is a JSON object with exactly one key: `reason` |
| **Test_4** | Asserts the `reason` property is a string with value `"Bad credentials"` |
| **Test_5** | Clears the `token` collection variable by setting it to an empty string, ensuring no stale token is used in subsequent requests |

# Auth - CreateToken (Missing Password)

This request attempts to authenticate against the RESTful Booker API with only a `username` field — the `password` field is intentionally omitted from the request body. The API returns `200 OK` even in this case, but instead of a token, the response contains a `reason` field with the value `"Bad credentials"`. The `token` collection variable is cleared (set to empty string) to prevent stale tokens from being used in subsequent requests.

---

## Endpoint

```
POST {{base_url}}/auth

 ```

---

## Authentication

No authentication is required for this request.

---

## Request Headers

| Header | Value |
| --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` |

---

## Request Body

The request body contains only the `username` field. The `password` field is **intentionally missing** to test the API's handling of incomplete credentials.

``` json
{
  "username": "{{valid_username}}"
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Variable | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `username` | string | `{{valid_username}}` | A valid username — but authentication still fails due to the missing password |
| `password` | — | _(omitted)_ | Intentionally not included in the request body |

---

## Response

### 200 OK

Despite the missing `password` field, the API returns `200 OK`. The response body contains a single `reason` field instead of a token.

``` json
{
  "reason": "Bad credentials"
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `reason` | string | Explains why authentication failed. Value is `"Bad credentials"` when the password is missing |

---

## Variables

Upon receiving the response, the `token` collection variable is **cleared** (set to an empty string) to ensure no stale token from a previous successful authentication is accidentally used in subsequent requests.

---

## Notes

- The API does **not** return a `4xx` error for a missing password — it always returns `200 OK`.
    
- Even a valid `username` is not sufficient — both fields are required for successful authentication.
    
- Response time is validated against the `max_response_time` collection variable.
    
- The response body is validated to be a JSON object with exactly one key: `reason`.
    

---

## Automated Tests

| Test | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Test_1** | Asserts the response status code is `200 OK` (API returns 200 even for missing password) |
| **Test_2** | Asserts the response time is below the `{{max_response_time}}` collection variable value |
| **Test_3** | Asserts the response body is a JSON object with exactly one key: `reason` |
| **Test_4** | Asserts the `reason` property is a string with value `"Bad credentials"` |
| **Test_5** | Clears the `token` collection variable by setting it to an empty string, ensuring no stale token is used in subsequent requests |

item.description:
## Overview

This folder covers the authentication endpoint (`POST /auth`) of the RESTful Booker API. Its purpose is to obtain a session token using valid credentials. The token is required to perform protected operations — specifically **updating** (`PUT`, `PATCH`) and **deleting** (`DELETE`) bookings under `/booking/:id`.

In addition to the happy-path token creation, this folder includes **negative test cases** that verify the API's behavior when invalid or incomplete credentials are submitted.

---

## Endpoint

| Method | Endpoint |
|--------|----------|
| `POST` | `{{restful-booker_url}}/auth` |

---

## Requests in this Folder

| # | Name | Scenario | Expected Outcome |
|---|------|----------|------------------|
| 1 | `create_token_valid_user_and_password` | Valid credentials | `200 OK` — returns `{"token": "..."}`, saved to `{{token}}` collection variable |
| 2 | `create_token_invalid_user_and_password` | Invalid username & password | `200 OK` — returns `{"reason": "Bad credentials"}`, `{{token}}` is cleared |
| 3 | `create_token_missing_password` | Missing password field | `200 OK` — returns `{"reason": "Bad credentials"}`, `{{token}}` is cleared |

---

## Token Usage

The token obtained from `create_token_valid_user_and_password` is stored in the `{{token}}` collection variable and passed as a `Cookie` header in the following protected endpoints:

```
Cookie: token={{token}}
```

| Endpoint | Operation |
|----------|-----------|
| `PUT /booking/:id` | Full update of an existing booking |
| `PATCH /booking/:id` | Partial update of an existing booking |
| `DELETE /booking/:id` | Delete an existing booking |

---

## API Behavior Notes

- The `/auth` endpoint **always returns `200 OK`** — even for invalid or incomplete credentials. No `4xx` errors are returned for authentication failures.
- Failed authentication returns `{"reason": "Bad credentials"}` in the response body instead of a token.
- On failed auth attempts, the `{{token}}` collection variable is **cleared** (set to an empty string) to prevent stale tokens from being used in subsequent requests.
- Response time is validated against the `{{max_response_time}}` collection variable in all requests within this folder.

request.description:
# GetBookingIds

Retrieves a list of all booking IDs currently stored in the system. Optionally, results can be filtered by guest name or check-in/check-out dates using query parameters.

This request is used in the **Workflow 1: Full CRUD Lifecycle** folder as a confirmation step — both after creating a new booking (to verify it appears in the list) and after deleting one (to verify it no longer appears).

---

## Request Details

| Property | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Method** | `GET` |
| **Endpoint** | `{{base_url}}/booking` |
| **Authentication** | None required |
| **Content-Type** | Not applicable |

---

## Query Parameters

All query parameters are **optional**. When omitted, all booking IDs are returned.

| Parameter | Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `firstname` | String | Filter by guest first name | `{{firstname}}` |
| `lastname` | String | Filter by guest last name | `{{lastname}}` |
| `checkin` | Date (`YYYY-MM-DD`) | Filter bookings with check-in on or after this date | `2026-02-12` |
| `checkout` | Date (`YYYY-MM-DD`) | Filter bookings with check-out on or before this date | `2026-02-15` |

---

## Example Response — 200 OK

``` json
[
  { "bookingid": 60 },
  { "bookingid": 223 },
  { "bookingid": 132 },
  { "bookingid": 480 }
]

 ```

---

## Expected Status Codes

| Status Code | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `200 OK` | Request succeeded; returns an array of booking ID objects |

---

## Test Coverage

| Test | Assertion |
| --- | --- |
| `Test_1` | Status code is `200` |
| `Test_2` | Response time is below `{{max_response_time}}` ms |
| `Test_3` | Response body is a non-empty array where each element contains an integer `bookingid` property |

---

## Variables Used

| Variable | Scope | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `{{base_url}}` | Collection | Base URL for the RESTful Booker API |
| `{{max_response_time}}` | Collection | Maximum acceptable response time in milliseconds |
| `{{firstname}}` | Collection | Guest first name used as an optional filter (disabled by default) |
| `{{lastname}}` | Collection | Guest last name used as an optional filter (disabled by default) |

---

# GetBookingIds (Filter by firstname)

Retrieves a list of booking IDs filtered by the guest's **first name**. Returns an array of objects, each containing a `bookingid` integer.

---

## Request

| Property | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Method | `GET` |
| Endpoint | `/booking` |
| Auth | None required |

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `firstname` | `string` | Yes | First name of the guest to filter bookings by. Resolved from the `{{firstname}}` collection variable. |

---

## Variables Used

| Variable | Scope | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `{{base_url}}` | Collection | Base URL of the RESTful Booker API (e.g. `https://restful-booker.herokuapp.com`) |
| `{{firstname}}` | Collection | First name of the guest used as the filter value |

---

## Response

### Success — `200 OK`

Returns a JSON array of booking ID objects matching the given first name.

**Response body shape:**

``` json
[
  { "bookingid": 1 },
  { "bookingid": 7 },
  { "bookingid": 42 }
]

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `bookingid` | `integer` | Unique identifier of a matching booking |

> If no bookings match the filter, an **empty array** `[]` is returned with a `200 OK` status. 
  

---

## Test Coverage

| Test | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Test_1` | Status code is `200` |
| `Test_2` | Response time is below `{{max_response_time}}` ms |
| `Test_3` | Response body is an array |
| `Test_4` | Each item in the array contains a numeric `bookingid` property (skipped if array is empty) |

---

## Notes

- If the `{{firstname}}` variable is not set, the query parameter will be sent as an empty string, which may return all bookings or an empty array depending on API behaviour.
    
- Use `get_all_bookingIds` to retrieve all booking IDs without any filter.

# GetBookingIds (Filter by firstname & lastname)

Returns a list of booking IDs filtered by both **firstname** and **lastname** query parameters. Use this endpoint when you need to look up bookings for a specific guest by their full name.

---

## Endpoint

```
GET {{base_url}}/booking?firstname={{firstname}}&lastname={{lastname}}
```

---

## Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `firstname` | string | Yes | First name of the guest who made the booking |
| `lastname` | string | Yes | Last name of the guest who made the booking |

---

## Authentication

No authentication required.

---

## Example Request

```
GET https://restful-booker.herokuapp.com/booking?firstname=John&lastname=Smith
```

---

## Response

### 200 OK — Bookings found

Returns an array of objects, each containing a `bookingid` integer.

```json
[
  { "bookingid": 1 },
  { "bookingid": 15 },
  { "bookingid": 42 }
]
```

### 200 OK — No matches

Returns an empty array when no bookings match the provided name.

```json
[]
```

---

## Variables Used

| Variable | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `{{base_url}}` | Base URL of the RESTful Booker API |
| `{{firstname}}` | First name filter value |
| `{{lastname}}` | Last name filter value |

---

## Test Coverage

| Test | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Test_1` | Status code is `200` |
| `Test_2` | Response time is below `max_response_time` collection variable |
| `Test_3` | Response body is an array |
| `Test_4` | Each item in the array contains a numeric `bookingid` property (skipped if array is empty) |

---

## Notes

- Both `firstname` and `lastname` must be provided together for a combined filter.
- If the array is empty, `Test_4` is automatically skipped and a warning is logged to the console.
- `bookingid` values are integers.

request.description:
# GetBookingIds (Filter by Check-in Date)

Retrieves a list of booking IDs filtered by a specific check-in date. This is a read-only request that queries the RESTful Booker API and returns all bookings whose check-in date matches the provided value.

---

## HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
GET {{base_url}}/booking?checkin={{checkin}}

 ```

---

## Query Parameters

| Parameter | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `checkin` | `{{checkin}}` | Date the guest is checking in (format: `YYYY-MM-DD`) |

---

## Successful Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

**Body:** A JSON array of booking ID objects. Each element has the following shape:

``` json
[
  { "bookingid": 1 },
  { "bookingid": 2 }
]

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `bookingid` | `number` (integer) | Unique identifier of the booking |

---

## Test Coverage

| Test | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Test_1` | Asserts the response status code is `200` |
| `Test_2` | Asserts the response time is below the `max_response_time` collection variable (parsed as an integer) |
| `Test_3` | Asserts the response body is an array |
| `Test_4` | Asserts each item in the array has a `bookingid` property that is an integer — **skipped if the array is empty** |

---

## Edge Cases

- **Empty array:** If no bookings match the given check-in date, the API returns an empty array `[]`. In this case, a warning is logged to the console (`console.warn`) and `Test_4` is skipped using `pm.test.skip` to avoid false failures.
    
- The `checkin` value must be a valid date string in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. Invalid or missing values may return an empty array or unexpected results.

# GetBookingIds (Filter by Check-in and Check-out Date)

Retrieves a list of booking IDs filtered by a specific check-in and check-out date. This is a read-only request that queries the RESTful Booker API and returns all bookings whose check-in and check-out date matches the provided value.

---

## HTTP Method & Endpoint

```
GET {{base_url}}/booking?checkin={{checkin}}&checkout={{checkout}}

 ```

---

## Query Parameters

| Parameter | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `checkin` | `2026-02-12` | Date the guest is checking in (format: `YYYY-MM-DD`) |
| `checkout` | {{checkout}\] | Date the guest is checking out (format: `YYYY-MM-DD`) |

---

## Successful Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

**Body:** A JSON array of booking ID objects. Each element has the following shape:

``` json
[
  { "bookingid": 1 },
  { "bookingid": 2 }
]

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `bookingid` | `number` (integer) | Unique identifier of the booking |

---

## Test Coverage

| Test | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Test_1` | Asserts the response status code is `200` |
| `Test_2` | Asserts the response time is below the `max_response_time` collection variable (parsed as an integer) |
| `Test_3` | Asserts the response body is an array |
| `Test_4` | Asserts each item in the array has a `bookingid` property that is an integer — **skipped if the array is empty** |

---

## Edge Cases

- **Empty array:** If no bookings match the given check-in date, the API returns an empty array `[]`. In this case, a warning is logged to the console (`console.warn`) and `Test_4` is skipped using `pm.test.skip` to avoid false failures.
    
- The `checkin` value must be a valid date string in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. Invalid or missing values may return an empty array or unexpected results.

## Overview

This folder contains all **GET** requests for reading booking data from the RESTful Booker API. It covers two main areas: listing/filtering booking IDs and fetching full booking details by ID.

---

### 📋 List & Filter Booking IDs

These requests retrieve a list of booking IDs from `GET /booking`, with optional query parameters to narrow results:

| Request | Query Parameters |
| --- | --- |
| `get_all_bookingIds` | _(none — returns all booking IDs)_ |
| `get_bookingIds_by_firstname` | `firstname={{firstname}}` |
| `get_bookingIds_by_lastname` | `lastname=Brown` |
| `get_bookingIds_by_firstname&lastname` | `firstname={{firstname}}&lastname={{lastname}}` |
| `get_bookingIds_by_checkin` | `checkin={{checkin}}` |
| `get_bookingIds_by_checkout` | `checkout={{checkout}}` |
| `get_bookingIds_by_checkin&checkout` | `checkin={{checkin}}&checkout={{checkout}}` |

---

### 🔍 Fetch Single Booking by ID

These requests retrieve full booking details from `GET /booking/{{bookingid}}`, covering both happy-path and edge cases:

- **`get_booking_by_valid_id`** — Fetches a booking using a valid, existing booking ID. Expects a `200 OK` with full booking details.
    
- **`get_booking_by_invalid_id`** — Uses a non-existent booking ID. Expects a `404 Not Found` response.
    
- **`get_booking_by_malformed_id`** — Uses a malformed/non-numeric ID. Validates API error handling for bad input.
    

---

> **Variables used:** `{{restful-booker_url}}`, `{{bookingid}}`, `{{firstname}}`, `{{lastname}}`, `{{checkin}}`, `{{checkout}}`

# Booking - CreateBooking (Valid)

This request creates a new booking in the RESTful Booker API using a valid, fully-populated request body. The `firstname`, `lastname`, and `totalprice` fields are randomly generated on each run using Postman dynamic variables. The remaining fields (`depositpaid`, `checkin`, `checkout`, `additionalneeds`) are hardcoded. On success, the API returns a `200 OK` with the new `bookingid` and the full `booking` object. The `bookingid` is automatically saved to the `bookingid` collection variable for use in subsequent requests.

---

## Endpoint

```
POST {{base_url}}/booking

 ```

---

## Authentication

No authentication is required to create a booking.

---

## Request Headers

| Header | Value |
| --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` |

---

## Pre-Request Script

Before the request is sent, the pre-request script:

- Generates a random `firstname` using `{{$randomFirstName}}`
    
- Generates a random `lastname` using `{{$randomLastName}}`
    
- Generates a random integer `totalprice` (0–1000) using `{{$randomInt}}`
    
- Uses hardcoded values for `depositpaid`, `checkin`, `checkout`, and `additionalneeds`
    
- Dynamically builds and sets the request body
    
- Saves all values to collection variables for use in post-response test validation
    

---

## Request Body

``` json
{
  "firstname": "{{firstname}}",
  "lastname": "{{lastname}}",
  "totalprice": {{totalprice}},
  "depositpaid": {{depositpaid}},
  "bookingdates": {
    "checkin": "{{checkin}}",
    "checkout": "{{checkout}}"
  },
  "additionalneeds": "{{additionalneeds}}"
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Source | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `firstname` | string | `{{$randomFirstName}}` | Randomly generated first name |
| `lastname` | string | `{{$randomLastName}}` | Randomly generated last name |
| `totalprice` | integer | `{{$randomInt}}` | Randomly generated price (0–1000) |
| `depositpaid` | boolean | Hardcoded: `false` | Whether the deposit has been paid |
| `bookingdates.checkin` | string | Hardcoded: `"2026-02-12"` | Check-in date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format |
| `bookingdates.checkout` | string | Hardcoded: `"2026-02-15"` | Check-out date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format |
| `additionalneeds` | string | Hardcoded: `"Breakfast"` | Any additional requirements |

---

## Response

### 200 OK

A successful response returns a JSON object with two properties: `bookingid` and `booking`.

``` json
{
  "bookingid": 1234,
  "booking": {
    "firstname": "John",
    "lastname": "Smith",
    "totalprice": 450,
    "depositpaid": false,
    "bookingdates": {
      "checkin": "2026-02-12",
      "checkout": "2026-02-15"
    },
    "additionalneeds": "Breakfast"
  }
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `bookingid` | integer | Unique ID of the newly created booking |
| `booking` | object | Full booking object echoing back the submitted data |
| `booking.firstname` | string | First name of the guest |
| `booking.lastname` | string | Last name of the guest |
| `booking.totalprice` | integer | Total price of the booking |
| `booking.depositpaid` | boolean | Whether the deposit has been paid |
| `booking.bookingdates.checkin` | string | Check-in date (`YYYY-MM-DD`) |
| `booking.bookingdates.checkout` | string | Check-out date (`YYYY-MM-DD`) |
| `booking.additionalneeds` | string | Additional requirements |

---

## Variables

| Variable | Scope | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `firstname` | Collection | Randomly generated — set by pre-request script, validated in tests |
| `lastname` | Collection | Randomly generated — set by pre-request script, validated in tests |
| `totalprice` | Collection | Randomly generated — set by pre-request script, validated in tests |
| `depositpaid` | Collection | Hardcoded `false` — set by pre-request script, validated in tests |
| `checkin` | Collection | Hardcoded `"2026-02-12"` — set by pre-request script, validated in tests |
| `checkout` | Collection | Hardcoded `"2026-02-15"` — set by pre-request script, validated in tests |
| `additionalneeds` | Collection | Hardcoded `"Breakfast"` — set by pre-request script, validated in tests |
| `bookingid` | Environment | Saved from the response — used in subsequent GET, PUT, PATCH, DELETE requests |

---

## Notes

- Response time is validated against the `max_response_time` collection variable.
    
- The `bookingid` is saved to the collection variable `bookingid` for use in downstream requests.
    
- Date fields are validated to match the `YYYY-MM-DD` format.
    
- `checkout` is validated to be strictly later than `checkin`.
    

---

## Automated Tests

| Test | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Test_1** | Asserts the response status code is `200 OK` |
| **Test_2** | Asserts the response time is below the `{{max_response_time}}` collection variable value |
| **Test_3** | Asserts the response body is a JSON object with exactly two keys: `bookingid` and `booking` |
| **Test_4** | Asserts `bookingid` is an integer and saves it to the `bookingid` collection variable |
| **Test_5** | Asserts `booking` is an object with exactly 6 keys: `firstname`, `lastname`, `totalprice`, `depositpaid`, `bookingdates`, `additionalneeds` |
| **Test_6** | Asserts `booking.firstname` is a string matching the generated `{{firstname}}` collection variable |
| **Test_7** | Asserts `booking.lastname` is a string matching the generated `{{lastname}}` collection variable |
| **Test_8** | Asserts `booking.totalprice` is an integer matching the generated `{{totalprice}}` collection variable |
| **Test_9** | Asserts `booking.depositpaid` is a boolean matching the hardcoded `{{depositpaid}}` collection variable (`false`) |
| **Test_10** | Asserts `booking.bookingdates` is an object with exactly 2 keys: `checkin` and `checkout` |
| **Test_11** | Asserts `bookingdates.checkin` is a non-empty string in `YYYY-MM-DD` format matching `{{checkin}}` |
| **Test_12** | Asserts `bookingdates.checkout` is a non-empty string in `YYYY-MM-DD` format matching `{{checkout}}` |
| **Test_13** | Asserts `checkout` date is strictly greater than `checkin` date |
| **Test_14** | Asserts `booking.additionalneeds` is a non-empty string matching the hardcoded `{{additionalneeds}}` collection variable |

# Booking - CreateBooking (Invalid date format)

This request tests the API's behavior when booking dates are provided in an **invalid format** (`DD-MM-YYYY` instead of the expected `YYYY-MM-DD`). The API is expected to reject such input and return an error response (`400` or `500`).

> ⚠️ **Known Bug**  
The API does not validate date formats and incorrectly returns `200 OK` instead of `400` or `500`. This is a bug that must be reported and fixed. 
  

---

## Endpoint

| --- | --- |
| `POST` | `{{base_url}} /booking` |

---

## Authentication

No authentication is required for this request.

---

| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Indicates the request body is JSON |

---

The following script dynamically generates values for `firstname`, `lastname`, `totalprice`, and `depositpaid` using Postman dynamic variables before each request execution:

``` javascript
const firstname = pm.variables.replaceIn('{{$randomFirstName}}')
const lastname = pm.variables.replaceIn('{{$randomLastName}}')
const totalprice = parseInt(pm.variables.replaceIn('{{$randomInt}}'), 10);

 ```

> These variables are then injected into the request body via `{{firstname}}`, `{{lastname}}` and `{{totalprice}}.`

``` json
{
    "firstname": "{{firstname}}",
    "lastname": "{{lastname}}",
    "totalprice": {{totalprice}},
    "depositpaid": false,
    "bookingdates": {
        "checkin": "12-02-2026",
        "checkout": "15-02-2026"
    },
    "additionalneeds": "Breakfast"
}
 ```

| `additionalneeds` | `string` | `"Breakfast"` | Static additional request |

---

## Expected Response

The tests expect the API to return a **`400`** or **`500`** error response, indicating that the invalid date format was rejected.

> ⚠️ **Bug:** The API currently returns `200 OK` along with a full booking object, which is **incorrect behavior**. The server should validate the date format and return an appropriate error response.

# Booking - CreateBooking (Checkout date before checkin)

This request tests the API's behavior when the **checkout date is earlier than the checkin date** — specifically, `checkout: 2026-02-12` before `checkin: 2026-02-15`. The API is expected to validate the date order and reject the request with a `400` or `500` error response.

> ⚠️ Known Bug  
The API does not validate date order and incorrectly returns `200 OK` instead of `400` or `500`. 
  

---

## Endpoint

| Method | URL |
| --- | --- |
| `POST` | `{{base_url}}/booking` |

---

## Authentication

No authentication is required for this request.

---

## Request Headers

| Key | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Sets the format of payload you are sending |

---

## Pre-Request Script

``` javascript
const firstname = pm.variables.replaceIn('{{$randomFirstName}}');
const lastname = pm.variables.replaceIn('{{$randomLastName}}');
const totalprice = parseInt(pm.variables.replaceIn('{{$randomInt}}'), 10);

 ```

This script dynamically generates request data before each run using Postman dynamic variables:

- **`firstname`** — a random first name generated via `{{$randomFirstName}}`
    
- **`lastname`** — a random last name generated via `{{$randomLastName}}`
    
- **`totalprice`** — a random integer generated via `{{$randomInt}}`, parsed to an integer
    

Note that `depositpaid` is hardcoded to `false` directly in the request body.

---

## Request Body

``` json
{
    "firstname": "{{firstname}}",
    "lastname": "{{lastname}}",
    "totalprice": {{totalprice}},
    "depositpaid": false,
    "bookingdates": {
        "checkin": "2026-02-15",
        "checkout": "2026-02-12"
    },
    "additionalneeds": "Breakfast"
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Value / Source | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `firstname` | String | `{{firstname}}` (pre-request script) | Randomly generated first name |
| `lastname` | String | `{{lastname}}` (pre-request script) | Randomly generated last name |
| `totalprice` | Integer | `{{totalprice}}` (pre-request script) | Randomly generated total price |
| `depositpaid` | Boolean | `false` (hardcoded) | Whether a deposit has been paid |
| `bookingdates.checkin` | String | `"2026-02-15"` (hardcoded) | Check-in date |
| `bookingdates.checkout` | String | `"2026-02-12"` ⚠️ (hardcoded) | **Checkout is before checkin** — this is the invalid scenario being tested |
| `additionalneeds` | String | `"Breakfast"` (hardcoded) | Any additional requirements for the booking |

---

## Expected Response

The test expects the API to return a **`400`** or **`500`** error response, since the provided date range is invalid (`checkout: 2026-02-12` is before `checkin: 2026-02-15`).

> ⚠️ **Known Bug:** The API currently returns `200 OK` with a full booking object, which is **incorrect behavior**. The server accepts and creates the booking without validating that the checkout date must be after the checkin date. 
  

---

| **Test_1. Status code indicates error** | Asserts that the response code is one of `[400, 500]`, indicating the API correctly rejected the invalid date range |
| **Test_2. Response time is below max_response_time** | Asserts that `responseTime` is below the integer value of the collection variable `max_response_time` |
| **Restore bookingid** | Sets the `bookingid` collection variable to an empty string `""` and asserts it equals `""`, ensuring a clean state for subsequent requests |

# UpdateBooking

Performs a **full replacement** of an existing booking record using the `PUT /booking/:id` endpoint of the RESTful Booker API. All booking fields are overwritten in a single request.

This request is used in the **full CRUD lifecycle workflow** as **Step 5**, immediately after the booking has been created and verified.

---

## Authentication

Authentication is provided via a **Cookie header**:

```
Cookie: token={{token}}

 ```

The `{{token}}` value is generated by the `create_token_valid_user_and_password` request and stored as a collection variable.

> **Alternative:** The `Authorization: Basic` header can also be used instead of the Cookie. See the commented-out code in the pre-request script (Step 3). 
  

---

## Variables Used

| Variable | Source | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `{{base_url}}` | Collection | Base URL of the RESTful Booker API |
| `{{bookingid}}` | Collection (set by `create_booking_valid`) | ID of the booking to update |
| `{{token}}` | Collection (set by `create_token_valid_user_and_password`) | Auth token for protected endpoints |
| `{{firstname}}` | Collection | Guest first name (unchanged in this update) |
| `{{updated_lastname}}` | Collection (set in pre-request script) | Randomly generated new last name |
| `{{totalprice}}` | Collection | Total price of the booking (unchanged) |
| `{{depositpaid}}` | Collection | Whether deposit was paid (unchanged) |
| `{{checkin}}` | Collection | Check-in date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format (unchanged) |
| `{{checkout}}` | Collection | Check-out date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format (unchanged) |
| `{{additionalneeds}}` | Collection | Additional needs string (unchanged) |

---

## Pre-request Script Behaviour

1. **Prerequisite check** — asserts that `{{bookingid}}` and `{{token}}` are already set as collection variables (fails fast if a prior step was skipped).
    
2. **Random lastname generation** — generates a new random last name using `{{$randomLastName}}` and saves it to `{{updated_lastname}}` for use in the request body and post-response assertions.
    

---

## Request Body

A full booking object is sent as JSON. Only `lastname` is intentionally changed; all other fields carry their original values:

``` json
{
    "firstname": "{{firstname}}",
    "lastname": "{{updated_lastname}}",
    "totalprice": {{totalprice}},
    "depositpaid": {{depositpaid}},
    "bookingdates": {
        "checkin": "{{checkin}}",
        "checkout": "{{checkout}}"
    },
    "additionalneeds": "{{additionalneeds}}"
}

 ```

---

## Expected Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

The response body mirrors the full updated booking object (6 fields):

``` json
{
    "firstname": "string",
    "lastname": "string (updated)",
    "totalprice": number,
    "depositpaid": boolean,
    "bookingdates": {
        "checkin": "YYYY-MM-DD",
        "checkout": "YYYY-MM-DD"
    },
    "additionalneeds": "string"
}

 ```

---

## Tests

| Test | Assertion |
| --- | --- |
| `Test_1` | Status code is `200` |
| `Test_2` | Response time is below `{{max_response_time}}` |
| `Test_3` | Response body is a valid JSON object with exactly 6 keys |
| `Test_4` | `firstname` is unchanged and matches the stored value |
| `Test_5` | `lastname` is updated and matches `{{updated_lastname}}` (differs from original `{{lastname}}`) |
| `Test_6` | `totalprice` is unchanged and is an integer |
| `Test_7` | `depositpaid` is unchanged and is a boolean |
| `Test_8` | `bookingdates` is an object with exactly 2 keys (`checkin`, `checkout`) |
| `Test_9` | `checkin` is unchanged, non-empty, and matches `YYYY-MM-DD` format |
| `Test_10` | `checkout` is unchanged, non-empty, and matches `YYYY-MM-DD` format |
| `Test_11` | `additionalneeds` is unchanged and matches the stored value |

# UpdateBooking — Cookie Header Disabled (Negative Test)

Attempts a **full booking update** (`PUT /booking/:id`) with the `Cookie` authentication header **intentionally disabled**. This is a **negative test** that verifies the API correctly rejects unauthenticated requests with a `403 Forbidden` response.

---

## Purpose

This request validates that the RESTful Booker API enforces authentication on the `PUT /booking/:id` endpoint. By disabling the `Cookie` header, the request is sent without any credentials, and the expected outcome is a `403 Forbidden` error.

---

## Authentication

| Method | Header | Status |
|--------|--------|--------|
| Cookie token | `Cookie: token={{token}}` | ⛔ **Disabled** |

> The `Cookie` header is present in the request but **disabled**. No `Authorization` header is set either. This simulates a call from an unauthenticated client.

---

## Request

**Method:** `PUT`  
**URL:** `{{base_url}}/booking/{{bookingid}}`

### Headers

| Key | Value | Enabled |
|-----|-------|---------|
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | ✅ Yes |
| `Cookie` | `token={{token}}` | ⛔ No |

### Body (raw JSON)

```json
{
    "firstname": "{{firstname}}",
    "lastname": "{{updated_lastname}}",
    "totalprice": {{totalprice}},
    "depositpaid": {{depositpaid}},
    "bookingdates": {
        "checkin": "{{checkin}}",
        "checkout": "{{checkout}}"
    },
    "additionalneeds": "{{additionalneeds}}"
}
```

---

## Required Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `base_url` | Environment | Base URL of the RESTful Booker API |
| `bookingid` | Collection | ID of the booking to update (set by a prior create step) |
| `token` | Collection | Auth token (unused here — header is disabled) |
| `firstname` | Collection | Guest first name |
| `updated_lastname` | Pre-request script | Randomly generated last name (set at runtime) |
| `totalprice` | Collection | Total price of the booking |
| `depositpaid` | Collection | Whether deposit has been paid (`true`/`false`) |
| `checkin` | Collection | Check-in date (`YYYY-MM-DD`) |
| `checkout` | Collection | Check-out date (`YYYY-MM-DD`) |
| `additionalneeds` | Collection | Any additional guest requirements |
| `max_response_time` | Collection | Maximum acceptable response time in ms |

---

## Pre-request Script Summary

1. **Prerequisite check** — Asserts that `bookingid` is set in collection variables before the request runs.
2. **Generate random last name** — Uses `{{$randomLastName}}` to produce a new `updated_lastname` value and stores it as a local variable for use in the body and post-response verification.
3. *(Commented out)* An alternative approach to inject the `Cookie` header programmatically via `pm.request.headers.add(...)` is included as a reference.

---

## Post-response Tests

| Test | Assertion |
|------|-----------|
| `Test_1` | Response status is `403 Forbidden` |
| `Test_2` | Response time is below `max_response_time` (ms) |
| `Test_3` | Response body is `text/plain` and equals `"Forbidden"` |

---

## Expected Response

**Status:** `403 Forbidden`  
**Content-Type:** `text/plain`  
**Body:**
```
Forbidden
```

---

## Notes

- This request is part of the **Bookings — Update Operations** folder and serves as a security/negative test case.
- To test the **successful** update flow, see `update_booking_valid` (which has a valid token in the Cookie header).
- The commented-out pre-request script block shows how to programmatically add the Cookie header as an alternative to setting it statically in the Headers tab.

# UpdateBooking — Invalid Token

Negative test case for the `PUT /booking/:id` endpoint. Sends a full booking update with a **deliberately invalid authentication token** in the `Cookie` header. The API is expected to reject the request with a `403 Forbidden` response, confirming that the endpoint enforces token validation before allowing any modifications.

---

## Request Details

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Method** | `PUT` |
| **Endpoint** | `{{base_url}}/booking/{{bookingid}}` |
| **Auth** | None (no auth helper) |
| **Content-Type** | `application/json` |

### Authentication

Authentication is passed via the `Cookie` header with a hardcoded invalid value:

```
Cookie: token=invalid_random_string

 ```

This simulates a scenario where a client presents a token that does not match any valid session on the server.

> **Note:** An alternative approach (commented out in the pre-request script) is to set the `Cookie` header programmatically via `pm.request.headers.add(...)`. 
  

---

## Request Body

A complete booking payload is sent despite the invalid token, to confirm the server rejects the request at the auth layer before processing the body:

``` json
{
    "firstname": "{{firstname}}",
    "lastname": "{{updated_lastname}}",
    "totalprice": {{totalprice}},
    "depositpaid": {{depositpaid}},
    "bookingdates": {
        "checkin": "{{checkin}}",
        "checkout": "{{checkout}}"
    },
    "additionalneeds": "{{additionalneeds}}"
}

 ```

---

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `base_url` | Collection | Base URL of the RESTful Booker API |
| `bookingid` | Collection | ID of the booking to update (set by a prior create request) |
| `firstname` | Collection | First name of the guest |
| `updated_lastname` | Pre-request script | Randomly generated last name (via `{{$randomLastName}}`) |
| `totalprice` | Collection | Total price of the booking |
| `depositpaid` | Collection | Whether a deposit has been paid |
| `checkin` | Collection | Check-in date |
| `checkout` | Collection | Check-out date |
| `additionalneeds` | Collection | Any additional needs for the booking |
| `max_response_time` | Collection | Maximum acceptable response time in milliseconds |

---

## Pre-request Script

1. **Prerequisite check** — Asserts that `bookingid` is set as a collection variable before the request is sent.
    
2. **Generate updated lastname** — Generates a random last name using `{{$randomLastName}}` and stores it in `updated_lastname`.
    
3. _(Commented out)_ Alternative method to inject the `Cookie` header programmatically instead of using the static header.
    

---

## Tests

| Test | Assertion |
| --- | --- |
| `Test_1` | Response status is `403 Forbidden` |
| `Test_2` | Response time is below `max_response_time` (collection variable) |
| `Test_3` | Response `Content-Type` is `text/plain`; body is not empty and equals `"Forbidden"` |

---

## Expected Response

**Status:** `403 Forbidden`

**Body:**

```
Forbidden

 ```

## Related Requests

- `update_booking_valid` — Same endpoint with a valid token; expects `200 OK`
    
- `update_booking_expired_token` — Same endpoint with an expired token; expects `403 Forbidden`
    
- `update_booking_invalid_id` — Same endpoint with an invalid booking ID; expects `405 Method Not Allowed`

# PartialUpdateBooking

Partially updates an existing booking by sending only the fields that need to change. Uses the HTTP `PATCH` method, which applies a partial modification — only the supplied fields are updated; all other fields retain their current values.

This request updates **`firstname`** **only** and verifies that all other booking fields remain unchanged.

---

## Authentication

# PartialUpdateBooking (Invalid date format)

This is a **negative test** for the `PATCH /booking/:id` endpoint. It submits booking dates in an **invalid format** (`DD/MM/YYYY` instead of the required `YYYY-MM-DD`) and verifies how the API handles the malformed input.

Despite the invalid date format, the RESTful Booker API currently returns **200 OK** — this request documents and validates that (potentially unexpected) behaviour.

---

## Endpoint

`PATCH {{base_url}}/booking/{{bookingid}}`

---

## Purpose

- Verify API behaviour when `checkin` and `checkout` dates are submitted in an incorrect format (`DD/MM/YYYY`).
    
- Confirm that only `firstname` is partially updated; all other fields remain unchanged.
    
- Assert that the response dates do **not** conform to the expected `YYYY-MM-DD` format — exposing the API's lack of input validation on date fields.
    

---

## Authentication

Uses a session token passed via the `Cookie` header:

```
Cookie: token={{token}}

 ```

The `{{token}}` variable must be set before this request runs (e.g. by executing `create_token_valid_user_and_password` first).

---

## Request Body

``` json
{
    "bookingdates": {
        "checkin": "12/02/2026",
        "checkout": "15/02/2026"
    }
}

 ```

> ⚠️ Dates are intentionally in `DD/MM/YYYY` format — this is the invalid input being tested. 
  

---

## Required Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `base_url` | Collection | Base URL of the RESTful Booker API |
| `bookingid` | Collection | ID of the booking to partially update |
| `token` | Collection | Auth token from the `/auth` endpoint |
| `partial_updated_firstname` | Collection | Expected `firstname` value after partial update |
| `lastname` | Collection | Original `lastname` (should remain unchanged) |
| `totalprice` | Collection | Original `totalprice` (should remain unchanged) |
| `depositpaid` | Collection | Original `depositpaid` (should remain unchanged) |
| `checkin` | Collection | Original `checkin` date (should remain unchanged) |
| `checkout` | Collection | Original `checkout` date (should remain unchanged) |
| `additionalneeds` | Collection | Original `additionalneeds` (should remain unchanged) |
| `max_response_time` | Collection | Maximum acceptable response time in ms |

---

## Pre-request Script

Validates that `bookingid` and `token` collection variables are set before the request is sent.

---

## Tests (Post-response)

| Test | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Test_1` | Status code is `200` |
| `Test_2` | Response time is below `max_response_time` |
| `Test_3` | Response body is a valid JSON object with exactly 6 expected keys |
| `Test_4` | `firstname` reflects the partially updated value (`partial_updated_firstname`) |
| `Test_5` | `lastname` is unchanged |
| `Test_6` | `totalprice` is unchanged |
| `Test_7` | `depositpaid` is unchanged |
| `Test_8` | `bookingdates` is an object with `checkin` and `checkout` keys |
| `Test_9` | `checkin` matches `YYYY-MM-DD` format and equals the stored value _(expected to_ _**fail**_ _due to invalid input)_ |
| `Test_10` | `checkout` matches `YYYY-MM-DD` format and equals the stored value _(expected to_ _**fail**_ _due to invalid input)_ |
| `Test_11` | `additionalneeds` is unchanged |

> ℹ️ **Tests 9 and 10 are expected to fail** — this is intentional. They document the API's failure to reject or normalise invalid date formats. 
  

---

``` json
{
    "firstname": "{{partial_updated_firstname}}",
    "lastname": "{{lastname}}",
    "totalprice": {{totalprice}},
    "depositpaid": {{depositpaid}},
    "bookingdates": {
        "checkin": "<invalid or unexpected format>",
        "checkout": "<invalid or unexpected format>"
    },
    "additionalneeds": "{{additionalneeds}}"
}

 ```

item.description:
## Overview

This folder covers test scenarios for the **Update Booking** (`PUT /booking/:id`) and **Partial Update Booking** (`PATCH /booking/:id`) endpoints of the RESTful Booker API.

It validates both the **happy path** and a range of **negative/edge cases**, organized as follows:

**PUT – Full Update**

- `update_booking_valid` — Full update with a valid auth token; verifies all fields are correctly updated in the response.
    
- `update_booking_cookie_header_disabled` — Verifies that a request without the Cookie auth header is rejected.
    
- `update_booking_expired_token` — Verifies that an expired token results in a `403 Forbidden` response.
    
- `update_booking_invalid_token` — Verifies that an invalid/random token is rejected with `403 Forbidden`.
    
- `update_booking_invalid_id` — Verifies that attempting to update a non-existent booking ID returns the appropriate error.
    

**PATCH – Partial Update**

- `partial_update_booking_valid` — Partial update (firstname only) with a valid token; verifies only the targeted field is changed.
    
- `partial_update_booking_cookie_header_disabled` — Verifies that a PATCH request without auth is rejected.
    
- `partial_update_booking_expired_token` — Verifies that an expired token results in a `403 Forbidden` response.
    
- `partial_update_booking_invalid_token` — Verifies that an invalid token is rejected with `403 Forbidden`.
    
- `partial_update_booking_invalid_date_format` — Verifies that submitting booking dates in an incorrect format returns an error.
    
- `partial_update_booking_invalid_id` — Verifies that a PATCH request to a non-existent booking ID returns the appropriate error.
    

Authentication is handled via the `Cookie: token={{token}}` header. Pre-request scripts generate dynamic test data (e.g. random names), and test scripts assert status codes, response structure, and field-level correctness.

Deletes an existing booking by its ID. This is a **protected endpoint** — a valid session token must be provided via the `Cookie` header.

Returns `201 Created` with a plain-text body of `Created` on success.

---

## Authentication

Authentication is passed via the `Cookie` header:

```
Cookie: token={{token}}

 ```

The `{{token}}` collection variable must be populated before this request runs (e.g. by executing `create_token_valid_user_and_password` first).

> **Alternative:** The commented-out code in the pre-request script shows how to set the `Cookie` header programmatically instead of using a static header.

# DeleteBooking (Cookie Header Disabled)

Attempts to delete an existing booking **without** a valid `Cookie` authentication header. The `Cookie` header is present in the request but **disabled**, so no token is sent. This is a **negative / security test** that verifies the API correctly rejects unauthenticated delete requests with a `403 Forbidden` response.

---

## Endpoint

`DELETE {{base_url}}/booking/{{bookingid}}`

---

## Purpose

Validates that the RESTful Booker API enforces authentication on the `DELETE /booking/:id` endpoint. When the `Cookie: token=` header is omitted or disabled, the API must refuse the request and return `403 Forbidden`.

---

## Authentication

| Header | Value | Status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | ✅ Enabled |
| `Cookie` | `token={{token}}` | ❌ Disabled |

> **Note:** The `Cookie` header is intentionally disabled to simulate a request made without authentication. To send an authenticated delete request, enable the `Cookie` header or use the `Authorization` header alternative (see pre-request script comments). 
  

---

## Required Variables

| Variable | Scope | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `base_url` | Collection | Base URL of the RESTful Booker API |
| `bookingid` | Collection | ID of the booking to delete |
| `token` | Collection | Auth token obtained from `POST /auth` |
| `max_response_time` | Collection | Maximum acceptable response time (ms) |

---

## Pre-request Script

Validates that `bookingid` and `token` collection variables are set before the request is sent. Also includes a commented-out alternative for programmatically adding the `Cookie` header via script.

---

## Tests

| Test | Assertion |
| --- | --- |
| `Test_1` | Response status is `403 Forbidden` |
| `Test_2` | Response time is below `max_response_time` |
| `Test_3` | Response body is `"Forbidden"` (plain text, non-empty) |

---

## Expected Response

**Status:** `403 Forbidden`

**Content-Type:** `text/plain`

**Body:**

```
Forbidden

 ```

# DeleteBooking (Expired Token)

This request is a **negative/security test** that verifies the API correctly rejects a delete operation when an **expired or invalid authentication token** is provided. The expected outcome is a `403 Forbidden` response, confirming that the server enforces token validity before allowing destructive operations.

---

## Method & Endpoint

| Method | Endpoint |
| --- | --- |
| `DELETE` | `{{base_url}}/booking/{{bookingid}}` |

---

## Authentication

Authentication is provided via a hardcoded **expired token** in the `Cookie` header:

```
Cookie: token=ccb59d3efd94c3a

 ```

This token is **intentionally expired/invalid** to trigger the `403 Forbidden` response. No valid token is used in this request by design.

---

## Headers

| Header | Value |
| --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` |
| `Cookie` | `token=ccb59d3efd94c3a` |

---

## Variables Used

| `{{bookingid}}` | ID of the booking to be deleted (set by a prior create request) |

- **Validates** that the `bookingid` collection variable is defined and not `undefined`. If this check fails, it signals that the test environment is not properly set up and the request should not proceed.
    

---

Three automated tests run after the response is received:

| Test | Assertion |
| --- | --- |
| **Test_1** | Response status code is `403` (Forbidden) |
| **Test_2** | Response time is below the `max_response_time` collection variable (in ms) |
| **Test_3** | Response `Content-Type` is `text/plain`, body is not empty, and body text equals `"Forbidden"` |

# DeleteBooking (Invalid Token)

This is a **negative test** that sends a `DELETE` request to `{{base_url}}/booking/{{bookingid}}` using an **intentionally invalid token** in the `Cookie` header. The goal is to verify that the API correctly rejects unauthorised deletion attempts and returns a `403 Forbidden` response.

---

## Endpoint

| Method | URL |
| --- | --- |
| `DELETE` | `{{base_url}}/booking/{{bookingid}}` |

---

## Authentication

No standard auth is configured. Instead, an invalid token is passed manually via the `Cookie` header:

| Header | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Cookie` | `token=invalid_random_string` | Intentionally invalid token to trigger a `403` response |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Declares the request body format |

> ⚠️ An alternative approach (commented out in the pre-request script) is to set the `Cookie` header programmatically via `pm.request.headers.add(...)`. 
  

---

## Pre-Request Script

The pre-request script performs a prerequisite check before the request is sent:

- **Step 1** — Asserts that the `bookingid` collection variable is set and not `undefined`. If this check fails, the test is aborted early to prevent false results.
    
- **Step 2** _(commented out)_ — An alternative way to inject the invalid token via `pm.request.headers.add(...)` instead of using a hardcoded header.
    

---

## Tests

| Test | Assertion |
| --- | --- |
| `Test_1. Response status is 403 (Forbidden)` | Response status code must be exactly `403` |
| `Test_2. Response time is below max_response_time` | Response time must be below the `max_response_time` collection variable (in milliseconds) |
| `Test_3. Response body is NOT empty` | `Content-Type` must be `text/plain`; body must not be empty and must equal `"Forbidden"` |

---

## Variables Used

| Variable | Scope | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `{{base_url}}` | Collection | Base URL for the RESTful Booker API |
| `{{bookingid}}` | Collection | ID of the booking to be deleted (set by a prior create request) |
| `{{max_response_time}}` | Collection | Maximum acceptable response time in milliseconds |

---

## Expected Behaviour

- The API must return `403 Forbidden` when an invalid token is provided.
    
- The response body must be the plain text string `"Forbidden"`.
    
- The response must arrive within the threshold defined by `{{max_response_time}}`.

request.description:
# DeleteBooking (Invalid Booking ID)

**Negative test** — attempts to delete a booking using a non-existent booking ID (`999999`) to verify that the API correctly rejects the request with a `405 Method Not Allowed` response. This test confirms the API's error-handling behaviour when an invalid resource ID is provided.

---

## Request Details

| Property | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Method** | `DELETE` |
| **URL** | `{{base_url}}/booking/{{bookingid}}` |

---

## Headers

| Header | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Specifies the request payload format |
| `Cookie` | `token={{token}}` | Passes the auth token for protected endpoint access; alternative to the `Authorization` header |

---

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `{{base_url}}` | Collection | Base URL of the RESTful Booker API |
| `{{bookingid}}` | Set in pre-request script | Overridden to `999999` to simulate a non-existent booking ID |
| `{{token}}` | Collection variable | Auth token passed via the `Cookie` header |
| `{{max_response_time}}` | Collection variable | Maximum acceptable response time threshold (in milliseconds) |

---

## Pre-request Script Behaviour

- **Step 1:** Sets `bookingid` to `999999` — a deliberately invalid/non-existent ID — to trigger an error response from the API.
    
- **Step 2 (commented out):** An alternative approach that manually constructs and adds the `Cookie` header using `pm.request.headers.add` with the token retrieved from `pm.collectionVariables.get("token")`. This can be enabled if the header-based approach is not sufficient.
    

---

## Expected Response

| Property | Expected Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Status code** | `405 Method Not Allowed` |
| **Content-Type** | `text/plain` |
| **Body** | `Method Not Allowed` |

---

## Test Coverage

| Test | Assertion |
| --- | --- |
| `Test_1` — Status code | Response status is `405 Method Not Allowed` |
| `Test_2` — Response time | Response time is below `{{max_response_time}}` (collection variable) |
| `Test_3` — Response body | Body is not empty, `Content-Type` is `text/plain`, and body equals `"Method Not Allowed"` |

item.description:
## Overview

This folder contains test cases for the `DELETE /booking/:id` endpoint of the Restful Booker API. The requests cover both the happy path and a range of failure/edge case scenarios to validate that the API correctly handles booking deletions under various authentication and input conditions.

**Endpoint:** `{{restful-booker_url}}/booking/{{bookingid}}`

**Authentication:** Requests require either a valid authorization token (via `Cookie` header) or a Basic Auth header.

### Scenarios Covered

| Request | Scenario |
| --- | --- |
| `delete_booking_valid` | Successful deletion using a valid authorization token |
| `delete_booking_invalid_token` | Deletion attempt with a malformed or incorrect token |
| `delete_booking_expired_token` | Deletion attempt using a token that has expired |
| `delete_booking_cookie_header_disabled` | Deletion attempt with the cookie/auth header intentionally disabled |
| `delete_booking_invalid_id` | Deletion attempt targeting a non-existent or invalid booking ID |
